summary: The tape gave it back. NQ ramped 380 points off a 28,961.25 overnight low into the open, tagged 29,342.50 in the first ninety minutes, then sold off every single hour into the close and finished 29,169, down 0.41%. That is a distribution afternoon. The context that matters: Alphabet and Tesla both reported after the bell today, and the fade was traders flattening ahead of the prints, not conviction selling. Volume backs that up, with RTH volume at 1,950k against a 2,440k six-session average, about 80% of normal. ES barely moved at -0.02%, which points at a tech-specific de-risk rather than a broad exit. Gold kept running, up 1.31% to a fresh 4,171.30 high before fading to 4,134.90. Crude closed up 2.28% and still flipped its 1hr stop short after reversing off an 88.61 overnight spike. Every index trend is technically intact, but NQ closed under its AVWAP for the first time in this leg. Not financial advice.
psychology:
Today punished anyone who needed the morning to keep going. The open was strong, the first two hours confirmed it, then it faded for six straight hours. If you added on the way up and defended on the way down, the problem was not the entry, it was deciding how the day would end before the day ended.

Tonight's specific habit to watch: two mega-cap names just reported into a market you are holding positions in. The reaction is unpriced and it will show up as a gap, and a gap does not fill your stop at your stop. The question is not whether you are right about the trend, it is what size you can hold through a print you cannot see. If the honest answer is smaller than what you are holding, that is the trade to make. Most of the money that survives earnings season is saved the night before, not made the morning after.

event.name: Alphabet (GOOGL) and Tesla (TSLA) Q2 results, released after the July 22 cash close
event.type: POST-EVENT PENDING (corporate earnings, no scheduled US macro release during the session)
event.consensus: n/a (corporate earnings, not a macro print)
event.actual: Released after the 20:00 UTC cash close; market reaction lands in the overnight session
event.surprise: Unpriced as of the RTH close. The session shows positioning ahead of the print, not a reaction to it.
event.nqReaction: Textbook pre-event de-risking. MNQ ramped hard overnight, opened 29,276.75, made its 29,342.50 high within ninety minutes, then bled lower on every hour into a 29,169 close, down 0.41%. It did this on 0.80x the six-session average volume. Heavy selling on light volume is usually absence of buyers rather than presence of sellers, which is what you expect when the market is waiting on information.
event.crossAsset: The split is the signal. ES finished flat at -0.02% while NQ lost 0.41%, pointing at a tech-specific unwind rather than broad risk-off. Gold rose 1.31% to a new 4,171.30 high, consistent with hedging demand. Crude closed up 2.28% but reversed hard from an 88.61 overnight spike and flipped its 1hr trend short, so the energy bid is losing grip even on a green day.

observe.name: ES/NQ confirmation
observe.note: The agreement established Jul 21 is still technically in place, both above their 1hr stops, but they diverged today (-0.02% versus -0.41%). Watch the MES 7,524.16 stop; a loss there breaks the alignment.

observe.name: Volume / liquidity axis
observe.note: Measured this run for the first time since Jul 17, closing the flag carried on Jul 20 and Jul 21. MNQ RTH volume 1,950k versus a 2,440k six-session average, a 0.80 ratio.

observe.name: GAP_NQ
observe.note: Outside the Net Alpha Pro stack. Context only.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ
observe.note: Outside the Net Alpha Pro stack. Context only.

risk.ddProximity: Not binding. Book entered flat, full daily budget intact.
risk.dailyLossBudget: Full budget intact at the REDUCED band.
risk.sizing: Reduce overnight exposure specifically. Intraday sizing on tomorrow's post-open setups can stay at the REDUCED band. MNQ ATR(14) rose to 99.26 from 93.68, so the volatility input is higher; the stop being far away is a reason for smaller size, not larger. Lines that must hold for the long thesis: MNQ 29,077.40 / MES 7,524.16 / MGC 4,122.27. MCL has already flipped and is short below 88.36.
risk.openRisk: The correlated long stack from Jul 21 has partially broken up, which cuts correlation risk but adds conflict risk. Crude flipping short while gold makes new highs and the indices stall is a mixed-signal regime. Mixed signals are a reason to trade less, not to find something to trade.
risk.note: Escalated from NORMAL to REDUCED on three stacked conditions: unpriced overnight earnings reaction from Alphabet and Tesla, a fade on 0.80x normal volume, and MNQ sitting between its AVWAP and its stop, the least informative zone on the chart. Earnings gaps do not respect stops, so only carry what you would willingly hold through an unknown gap. Week ahead: jobless claims 08:30 ET Thursday land inside the COMPASS opening-range window, treat the first fifteen minutes as contaminated. Intel and IBM report Thursday. Friday July 24 brings July flash PMIs 09:45 ET and June New Home Sales 10:00 ET. FOMC is July 28 to 29.

tomorrowsMap.if: MNQ reclaims the 29,247.36 AVWAP on 2 hourly closes
tomorrowsMap.then: today's fade is just a pullback and the 29,342.50 RTH high, then the 29,346.46 +2s, come back into scope
tomorrowsMap.bias: Long only on the reclaim. Below the AVWAP this is a lower-high tape reacting to earnings, and the burden is on the buyers

tomorrowsMap.if: MNQ closes below the 29,119.00 RTH low on 2 hourly closes
tomorrowsMap.then: the 29,098.71 -3s and then the 29,077.40 stop are the next tests, and a close under the stop ends the long trend
tomorrowsMap.bias: The stop is the line, not the low. Between 29,119.00 and 29,077.40 is still give-back. The -3s and the stop sit 21 points apart, so that zone is the real decision

tomorrowsMap.if: MES closes below the 7,524.16 stop on 2 hourly closes
tomorrowsMap.then: the long flip that started Jul 21 is void and the 7,503.25 overnight low opens up
tomorrowsMap.bias: ES is the tell tomorrow. It held flat while NQ fell, so if ES cracks too the de-risk is broad rather than tech-only

tomorrowsMap.if: MGC reclaims the 4,148.74 AVWAP on 2 hourly closes
tomorrowsMap.then: the 4,171.30 RTH high and then the 4,200 psych are live again
tomorrowsMap.bias: Long above the 4,122.27 stop. Gold made a new high and still closed 13.84 under its AVWAP, so reclaiming it is the first job

tomorrowsMap.if: MCL closes back above the 88.36 stop on 2 hourly closes
tomorrowsMap.then: the short flip is void and the 88.61 overnight high is the retest
tomorrowsMap.bias: Short below the stop while the daily change and the trend signal disagree. Headline-driven, so size for gap risk or stand aside

yesterdaysMapScore.note: Every row triggered, which is unusual, and the two mixed grades share one cause. Both the MNQ and MES upside rows fired on the trigger but died on follow-through because the afternoon reversed. The map read direction correctly and overestimated extension.

fullReport.title: Market Regime Snapshot (6-Axis)
fullReport.html:
AxisStateConfidenceEvidence
DirectionTREND UP, WEAKENINGMediumMNQ holds above its 29,077.40 stop but closed under both the 29,247.36 AVWAP and the 29,290 prior close. Cross-asset is split: MNQ -0.41%, MES -0.02%, MGC +1.31%, MCL +2.28%.
VolatilityELEVATED, EXPANDINGHighMNQ ATR(14) 99.26, up from 93.68 on Jul 21. RTH range 223.50 points. RTH sigma 49.55.
SessionOPEN DRIVE THEN DISTRIBUTIONHigh380-point overnight ramp into a 29,276.75 open, 29,342.50 high by 15:00 UTC, then lower on every subsequent hourly bar to close 29,169.
InventoryOVERNIGHT LONG, LIQUIDATEDMediumLongs built through the overnight ramp and were distributed all afternoon. Close in the lower third of the RTH range resets inventory toward balanced.
EventPOST-EVENT PENDINGHighAlphabet and Tesla reported after the cash close. The reaction is unpriced and arrives overnight. Intel and IBM Thursday; jobless claims Thursday 08:30 ET.
LiquidityTHIN, BELOW AVERAGEMediumMeasured this run: MNQ RTH volume 1,950k against a 2,440k six-session average, a 0.80 ratio. Jul 21 was similarly light at 1,907k. Only six prior sessions in the base, so Medium rather than High.

fullReport.title: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (13:30 to 13:44 UTC)
fullReport.html:
SymbolOR HighOR LowOR WidthBreakTime UTCPriceOutcome
MNQ29,284.2529,089.75194.50UP15:0529,293.00Failed. Price returned inside the range and closed 29,169, a Back Into Range result.
MES7,552.007,525.0027.00UP15:207,556.00Extended to 7,563.00 then faded back inside to close 7,541.00.
MGC4,168.904,136.7032.20UP15:454,169.60Marginal break, high 4,171.30, then reversed to close 4,134.90, back inside the range.
MCL87.0786.180.89UP14:1087.11Broke early, extended to 87.33, then faded to close 86.49, back inside the range.

All four broke their opening range to the upside and all four closed back inside it. That is a uniform failed-breakout day and the single most useful structural fact in this report.

fullReport.title: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter)
fullReport.html: No qualifying long. The E3v2 regime filter requires an expanding trend to accept a 15-minute breakout, and today delivered the opposite: the one upside break (OR high at 15:05 UTC) failed within the hour and price closed 115 points below the break level. The failed break is the anti-signal, and it argued for the range and reversion plays instead of continuation.

fullReport.title: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ)
fullReport.html:
SymbolAVWAPSigma+1s+2s+3s-1s-2s-3sClose pos
MNQ29,247.3649.5529,296.9129,346.4629,396.0129,197.8129,148.2629,098.71-1.58s
MES7,548.177.587,555.757,563.337,570.917,540.597,533.017,525.43-0.94s
MGC4,148.7410.124,158.864,168.984,179.104,138.624,128.504,118.38-1.37s
MCL86.600.2486.8487.0887.3286.3686.1285.88-0.46s

No instrument closed at a band extreme, so there is no live reversion trigger. All four closed below their AVWAP between the -0.5s and -1.6s zone, the drifting-away-but-not-stretched state. That is the least tradeable part of the band, and the correct read is to wait rather than anticipate.

fullReport.title: Strategy Habitat Check
fullReport.html:
StrategyHabitatRead
AVWAP MidrangeIN_HABITATPrice spent the entire afternoon between the 29,247.36 AVWAP and the 29,089.75 OR low, exactly the band this edge trades. The 194.50 OR width gave the range room to be worth playing.
AVWAP Back Into RangeIN_HABITATThe cleanest fit today. The OR broke up at 15:05 UTC to 29,293.00, then price closed back inside the 29,284.25 OR high and kept going, finishing 29,169 well within the range.
TSMOM-20BORDERLINEThe 1hr trend is still long with price 91.60 above the stop, so the signal has not flipped. But the AVWAP loss, expanding ATR and thin volume argue the trend is losing its footing.
AVWAP Deviation BandBORDERLINEMNQ closed at -1.58 sigma, inside the -1s and -2s markers but short of a band trigger. The -2s at 29,148.26 is the first reversion-long reference.
AVWAP Hand-offBORDERLINEShort-only, and the afternoon had the right shape: an OR break up that failed, then a slide down through the AVWAP. Borderline because the hand-off wants break and continuation in the same direction.

Out of habitat today: AVWAP Bounce (price closed 78.36 below the AVWAP rather than respecting it as support) and E3v2 Momentum Long (the regime filter gates off a long breakout in a fading, below-average-volume tape).

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked
fullReport.html:
#Row (as published Jul 21)StatusGradeWhat happened
1MNQ holds above the 29,291.62 AVWAP on hourly closesTRIGGEREDMIXEDHeld for exactly two hours (closes 29,330.25 and 29,311.00), then broke. No upside reference printed: high stopped at 29,342.50, twenty-one points short of the 29,363.50 first target. Closed 29,169. The row's risk framing was right: the first real test was the AVWAP loss, not the stop, and the 29,079.83 stop was never touched.
2MNQ closes below the 29,236.68 -1s on 2 hourly closesTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUTFired on consecutive closes of 29,161.00 and 29,169.00. The 29,063.00 RTH low came into scope with today's low at 29,119.00 and the stop came within 42 points. The bias line called it: give-back, not a trend change. Stop held with 91.60 to spare.
3MES closes above the 7,554.25 RTH high / 7,567.27 +3s on 2 hourly closesTRIGGEREDMIXEDThe 7,554.25 limb triggered on closes of 7,560.00 and 7,561.25 and ES printed a fresh 7,563.00 high. The +3s was never closed above. The record retest did not develop; price faded to 7,541.00 and the 7,620.90 cash ATH finished 79.90 away. Flip not voided, stop ratcheted to 7,524.16.
4MGC closes above the 4,092.20 RTH high / 4,092.34 +2sTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUTCleared both on the first RTH hour. The 4,100 psych fell, gold ran to a 4,171.30 high, the blue sky the row described. Closed 4,134.90, up 1.31%. The 4,052.71 stop was never threatened.
5MCL holds above the 84.44 AVWAP on hourly closesTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUTHeld every hourly close; session low 85.78. Both targets cleared, and crude ran to 88.61 overnight. Closed 86.49, up 2.28%. The gap-risk caveat earned its place: that spike fully reversed and the 1hr stop flipped short intraday even on a green day.

Score: 5 rows, 5 triggered, 3 played out, 0 failed, 2 mixed. Every row triggered, which is unusual, and the two mixed grades share one cause. Both the MNQ and MES upside rows fired on the trigger but died on follow-through because the afternoon reversed. The map read direction correctly and overestimated extension. MTD: 50 triggered / 28 played out / 5 failed.

COMPASS filter (Thu Jul 23): initial jobless claims print at 13:30 UTC, directly inside the 13:30 to 13:44 UTC opening-range window. Treat the opening range as event-contaminated and wait for the post-release range to settle before taking a COMPASS session entry. Today's MNQ OR width was 194.50, roughly 2.0x the 99.26 ATR(14), a wide-range open; wide-OR days have favored the Back Into Range and Midrange plays over OR-break continuation.

Calendar: Thursday July 23 brings initial jobless claims at 08:30 ET, plus Intel and IBM earnings. Alphabet and Tesla reported after Wednesday's close, so that reaction hits the overnight session and Thursday's open. Friday July 24 has July flash PMIs at 09:45 ET and June New Home Sales at 10:00 ET (carried from the Jul 21 verified calendar). FOMC is July 28 to 29.
